Documentation.short_help: Optimize an existing tetrahedral mesh with TetGen (no new vertices).
Documentation.long_help: Takes a vtkUnstructuredGrid containing tetrahedra (VTK_TETRA). Non-tetrahedral cells are ignored. TetGen reconstructs the mesh and runs mesh improvement. Refinement is capped with -S0 and boundary splits with -Y so Delaunay refinement does not add Steiner points. Target Max Aspect Ratio defaults to 20 (TetGen -o//): TetGen's internal default is 1000, which rarely marks any tet as bad, so the mesh looked unchanged—lower values make flip-based improvement actually run. Set it to 1000 to restore TetGen's loose default. Default Extra Improve Iterations is 0 (flip-only); raising it enables TetGen's smoother/Steiner-capable loop and may insert vertices. Preserve Unused Vertices (-J) avoids dropping input nodes not used by any tet.

IntVectorProperty.name: OptMaxFlipLevel
IntVectorProperty.label: Max Flip Level (-O#)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: TetGen -O# maximum flip link level during optimization.

IntVectorProperty.name: OptScheme
IntVectorProperty.label: Optimization Scheme (-O/#)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: TetGen -O/# bit mask for optimization operations.

DoubleVectorProperty.name: OptMaxAspectRatio
DoubleVectorProperty.label: Target Max Aspect Ratio (-o//)
DoubleVectorProperty.Documentation: Upper bound on tet aspect ratio for improvement (TetGen -o//). Lower = more tets treated as bad and more flip work (typical 10–40). Use 1000 for TetGen's default (usually almost no visible change).

IntVectorProperty.name: OptIterations
IntVectorProperty.label: Extra Improve Iterations (-O//#)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: TetGen -O//#: iterations of the secondary improvement loop (smoothing and optional Steiner insertion). Default 0 keeps only the flip-only pass and avoids new vertices from mesh improvement. Increase only if you accept possible Steiner points from TetGen in that phase.

IntVectorProperty.name: DoCheck
IntVectorProperty.label: Check Mesh (-C)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: Run TetGen consistency check on the result.

IntVectorProperty.name: NoJettison
IntVectorProperty.label: Preserve Unused Vertices (-J)
IntVectorProperty.Documentation: When ON, TetGen does not jettison unused input vertices from the output.
